items.title: Unauthenticated File Upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: A critical unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer for Magento
items.summary: Sansec discovered an unauthenticated PHP object injection flaw in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, allowing remote code execution via a crafted cookie. Mirasvit released a patched version (1.11.12) on May 25, 2026.

items.title: Security Update Available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This Adobe Security Bulletin addresses multiple critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, which could lead to unauthorized access, remote code execution, and service disruption.

items.title: Unrestricted File Upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files disguised as images, potentially leading to remote code execution. As of March 2026, no official patch was available for production versions, though a fix was included in the 2.4.9 pre-release branch.

items.title: Critical Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This critical security update addresses multiple vulnerabilities, including incorrect authorization (CVE-2026-21284), improper access control (CVE-2026-21285), stored XSS (CVE-2026-21310), and path traversal (CVE-2026-21293), which could lead to privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, or sensitive file access.
